content.problemStatement:
Chef has an array of integers where every element appears exactly twice, except for one unique element that appears only once. Chef wants to find this single unique integer. To help Chef, you need to implement an efficient solution that runs in linear time and uses only constant extra space. Help Chef identify the number that stands out from the duplicates so he can complete his task successfully.

Function Declaration

**Function Name**



findSingleNumberfindSingleNumberfindSingleNumber - This function identifies the unique integer in a list where every other integer appears twice.



**Parameters**



numbersnumbersnumbers ( Array(int)Array(int)Array(int) ): A reference to a vector of integers containing the input numbers.
The list contains integers where exactly one integer appears once, and all others appear twice.



**Return Value**



Returns an intintint representing the single number that appears only once in the input list.
The output is a single integer value that does not have a duplicate in the list.


Constraints
1≤T≤1051 \leq T \leq 10^51≤T≤105
−3×104≤numbers[i]≤3×104-3 \times 10^4 \leq numbers[i] \leq 3 \times 10^4−3×104≤numbers[i]≤3×104
Exactly one element appears once; all others appear twice.
Input Format
The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases.
For each test case:

The First line contains NNN integers — the elements of Chef's array.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing the integer that appears only once in Chef's array.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1: Numbers 7 and 3 appear twice; 9 appears once, so output is 9.
Test case 2: Numbers 10, 14, and 18 appear twice; 22 appears once, so output is 22.

Explanation:
Test case 1: Only one number, -10000, so output is -10000.
Test case 2: Numbers 0 and 0 appear twice, -30000 appears once, so output is -30000.
Test case 3: All numbers except 99 appear twice, so output is 99.

Explanation:
The numbers 1000, 2000, 3000, 4000, and 5000 each appear twice.
The number 7000 appears only once.
Hence, the output is 7000.
